channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Firefox and Windows zero-day security bugs hit by Russian hackers, so be on your guard
item.description: Russian hackers seen exploiting two zero-days to deploy backdoors against victims in Europe and North America.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: La IA facilita la labor investigadora de los científicos del Instituto Curie
item.description: Ciencia, investigación y descubrimiento. El Instituto Curie se fundó en 1909 y se estableció como un laboratorio de excelencia dirigido por la científica Marie Curie, una mujer pionera en su campo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft hits back at claims AI data scraping was sneakily turned on in Word, Excel
item.description: Microsoft said it does not use customer data to train LLMs, despite allegations from the creative community.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Taking stock of human capital in the age of AI
item.description: Turnover has always been a concern for employers, but in a recent PwC report, an increasing number of employees worldwide are looking to move within the next year in light of many things, but mostly because of opportunities afforded by the rapid pace of technological change.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Improving Multithreaded Performance with Cython 3.1
item.description: Multithreading Python with Cython's free-threaded mode, type annotations, and example usage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kotlin to lose scripting features
item.description: JetBrains plans to drop some scripting-related technologies from the Kotlin language, including REPL functionality, an obsolete API for Java scripting, the KotlinScriptMojo Maven plugin, and the kotlin-scripting-ide-services library for implementing code completion functionality.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Starbucks and other retailers hit with ongoing ransomware attack on software provider
item.description: Major supply-chain software provider Blue Yonder is working to restore its systems after a ransomware attack hit the Panasonic-owned firm last week. Blue Yonder, which counts Starbucks, major U.K.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Majority of social media influencers share information without verifying its accuracy: UN report
item.description: The majority of social media influencers share information with their followers without verifying its accuracy, according to a new U.N. report that was released on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe
item.description: To mitigate shadow AI, leaders should build cross-functional teams to leverage interdisciplinary expertise and foster organizational awareness. The post Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: This TikTok user says a popular YouTube music channel is in fact AI
item.description: Are you really a student if you’ve not done a late night study session soundtracked to an unending lo-fi beats playlist on YouTube? According to Google Trends, searches for the term “lo-fi” on YouTube have tripled since 2020.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Pentagon drafts large-scale government AI contract
item.description: Potential bidders have a little under a month to offer feedback on the draft RFP for what DoD is calling the Advancing AI Multiple Award Contract. The post Pentagon drafts large-scale government AI contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden administration finalizes nearly $8B in CHIPS funding for Intel
item.description: Intel will receive about $7.86 billion in funding from the CHIPS and Science Act to build new semiconductor manufacturing facilities across four states under its finalized agreement with the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Lauren Boebert Cameo page disappears amid House ethics concerns
item.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.) briefly offered videos on Cameo, a website on which public figures sell personalized videos to fans, before the page went offline amid questions about whether it would have violated House ethics rules.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Ask the CIO: Defense Commissary Agency
item.description: Learn how agencies are advancing their cybersecurity posture in today’s complex threat landscape. The post Ask the CIO: Defense Commissary Agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The biggest IT threat? That seemingly innocuous web browser
item.description: For decades, enterprises have allowed their workers to use whatever free browser they wanted to access the most sensitive files possible. CIOs believed that security software in the environment — such as endpoint security apps or supposedly secure web gateways — would deliver any needed protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The engine of Japan's flagship new small rocket explodes during a test for a second time
item.description: The engine for a flagship new small Japanese rocket has burst into flames during a combustion test, but there was no injury or damage to the outside, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Automaker spying on drivers, reporting data to insurance companies
item.description: Since April, seven class action lawsuits have been filed against General Motors, OnStar, and data brokers LexisNexis and Verisk Analytics. The suits accuse GM of partnering with data brokers to collect driving data without proper consent using OnStar Smart Driver technology.
item.dc:creator: Mike Colombo, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia’s new AI model Fugatto can generate music and audio
item.description: Nvidia on Monday showed a new artificial intelligence model for generating music and audio that can modify voices and generate novel sounds — technology aimed at the producers of music, films and video games.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Rethinking defense technology: A blueprint for US success in a complex global landscape
item.description: The Defense Department has made efforts to enhance its AI capabilities through the AI Adoption Strategy. The post Rethinking defense technology: A blueprint for US success in a complex global landscape first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Is Outlook down? Thousands of Microsoft 365 users report outage issues
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Thousands of Microsoft 365 customers worldwide reported having issues with services like Outlook and Teams on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meet the USC student rocket club that shattered a world altitude record
item.description: This could not be happening. Five years after becoming the first students to launch a rocket into space, the University of Southern California Rocket Propulsion Lab (USCRPL) was readying a redesigned model, Aftershock II, for an even higher ascent from Nevada’s Black Rock Desert.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Newsom says California will intervene if Trump kills $7,500 EV tax credit
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) said Monday the state of California will intervene if President-elect Trump eliminates the electric vehicle tax credit passed under President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Most young knowledge workers using AI: Poll
item.description: The vast majority of Generation Z knowledge workers said they are using artificial intelligence (AI) tools in their work, according to a new study, underscoring the increased embrace of the advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Orbia streamlines security across global sites with SASE
item.description: A push for greater network visibility and global security standards drove Orbia, an $8.2 billion company that operates in more than 100 countries, to deploy secure access service edge (SASE) technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Future of Data Streaming: Key Trends and Technologies
item.description: Explore the latest trends and innovations in data streaming technologies such as Kafka, Flink, and Iceberg.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Roblox gives parents more control over kids' experience
item.description: Roblox, which has been downloaded millions of times, announced sweeping and restrictive settings for minor safety.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: OpenAI is thinking about building its own browser
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly thinking about developing its own browser with the aim of challenging Google’s dominance in the market, according to The Information. The new browser would have built-in support for Chat GPT and Open AI’s search engine Search GPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI agents are unlike any technology ever
item.description: The agents are coming, and they represent a fundamental shift in the role artificial intelligence plays in businesses, governments, and our lives. The biggest news in agentic AI happened this month when we learned that OpenAI’s agent, Operator, is expected to launch in January.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to protect your deliveries from getting stolen by porch pirates
item.description: Porch pirates are on the prowl this holiday season. Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson has a number of tips to prevent holiday package thefts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
